summary: Updated Operator persona (ID: 90a7486f-46f9-41c9-a98c-21931fa5c5f6) from v2.2 to v3.0 with comprehensive v2 build orchestration flow. Replaced the legacy single-line BUILD TRIGGER with a detailed 7-step workflow covering pre-screen, initialization, Architect routing, brief presentation, worker execution, orchestrator check-in loop, and final close. Added explicit anti-pattern against /zo/ask for workers, documented the adaptive brief updating behavior, and referenced both the canonical system documentation and enforcement rule.
artifacts: Operator persona updated via edit_persona API
learnings: The previous BUILD TRIGGER was just 3 lines - far too minimal for the complexity of v2 orchestration
The orchestrator check-in loop (step 6) is the key differentiator - makes orchestrator intelligent rather than just a dispatcher
Important to reference both Documents/System/Build-Orchestrator-System.md and the enforcement rule for complete traceability
decisions: Bumped version to 3.0 (major change to build orchestration)
Kept all other persona sections intact (routing, Librarian integration, quality standards)
Added 'Build Orchestration' to Core Identity excel-at list
Added build status checks to 'I handle' list
Added explicit anti-pattern: 'Using /zo/ask for build workers'

notes_for_orchestrator.message: Version bumped to 3.0 due to major structural change in BUILD TRIGGER section. The persona now explicitly embeds the full v2 orchestration flow rather than just a 3-line stub.

When updating Architect persona, ensure it references the same script commands and emphasizes the worker brief creation workflow. The two personas should be complementary - Operator handles flow, Architect handles planning.
